concerns.text: Task T015 (QC/Motion exclusion) and T017 (Save metadata) are marked as incomplete (unchecked) but are listed AFTER T014 (Preprocess). T015 explicitly requires saving FD as a 'mandatory covariate column' in the output of T014. T017 saves the subject list and exclusion reasons derived from T015. The current order implies T014 runs before its own QC validation (T015) and metadata logging (T017) are complete, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule where the 'Preprocessed Data' artifact is not fully validated/annotated before the next phase (US2) consumes it. T015 and T017 must precede any task in US2 that consumes the preprocessed data.

Task T031 (Power analysis) is marked incomplete (unchecked) but is listed BEFORE T031b (Consume power calc) and T035 (Save statistical results). T031b explicitly depends on the 'minimum N required' value generated by T031. T035 depends on the outputs of T031 (power calc) and T029 (VIF/Ridge logic). The current ordering places the consumer tasks (T031b, T035) before the producer task (T031) is marked complete, creating a semantic dependency violation. T031 must be moved before T031b and T035.

Tasks T013b, T013c, and T013d are sub-tasks of T013 (Validate) but are listed AFTER T014 (Preprocess). T013c ('update model configuration to conditionally include detected confounders') and T013d ('log limitation') are validation/configuration steps that must occur BEFORE the preprocessing (T014) and subsequent analysis (US2/US3) can correctly utilize or account for these confounders. Placing configuration updates after the data processing step violates the 'Foundational tasks first' and 'Producer before consumer' principles, as the pipeline runs without the necessary configuration updates.

Task T013e (Check individual completeness, exclude subjects) is listed after T013b-d. While logically part of validation, the exclusion logic (T013e) must strictly precede T014 (Preprocess) to ensure no resources are wasted on excluded subjects. The current grouping suggests T013b-d (config/logging) might run before T013e (exclusion), which is semantically risky. T013e should be the final step of the validation block, immediately before T014.
